tasks.title: 项目初始化：Next.js 15 + TS strict + Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui
tasks.description: 使用 pnpm 创建 Next.js 15 App Router 项目，启用 TypeScript strict，集成 Tailwind CSS v4（@theme in CSS）和 shadcn/ui 基础组件，配置 ESLint/Prettier，搭建基础目录骨架（app/、lib/、components/、stores/、hooks/、tests/）。
tasks.details: 1) `pnpm create next-app@latest prisim-r2 --typescript --app --tailwind --eslint`；2) tsconfig 设 strict: true、noUncheckedIndexedAccess、noImplicitOverride；3) Tailwind v4 用 `@import 'tailwindcss';` 和 `@theme {}` 在 `app/globals.css`，不要 tailwind.config.js；4) `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest init`，添加 button/input/dialog/table/dropdown-menu/toast/tabs/badge/progress 等基础组件到 components/ui/；5) 安装 zustand、@tanstack/react-query、zod、ulid、@aws-sdk/client-s3、@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner（pin 精确版本）；6) 创建空目录：app/(auth)、app/(dashboard)、app/api/、lib/crypto、lib/r2、lib/db、lib/auth、lib/audit、lib/api、components/features、stores、hooks、tests/unit、tests/e2e；7) 在 .gitignore 添加 .dev.vars、.env.local、.wrangler/。
tasks.testStrategy: 运行 `pnpm dev` 应在 3000 端口起服务，访问 `/` 渲染默认页面；`pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint` 均无错误；shadcn 按钮组件能正常渲染。

subtasks.title: Create the Next.js 15 App Router project and enable strict TypeScript settings
subtasks.description: Scaffold the pnpm-based Next.js project, verify App Router setup, and harden tsconfig with the required strict compiler options.
subtasks.details:
Run `pnpm create next-app@latest prisim-r2 --typescript --app --tailwind --eslint` and enter the project directory with `cd prisim-r2`. Update `tsconfig.json` to ensure `"strict": true`, `"noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true`, and `"noImplicitOverride": true` are explicitly enabled under `compilerOptions`. Confirm the app uses the `app/` directory and keep the default Next.js 15 App Router structure intact.
<info added on 2026-05-20T11:37:41.607Z>
Implementation notes (2026-05-20):

Decision: manually scaffolded the project instead of running `pnpm create next-app@latest`, because the working tree already contained design mockups from a prior session (`app/globals.css` with Tailwind v4 tokens, `app/(auth)/login/page.tsx`, `components/layout/app-shell.tsx`, `components/features/{connections,upload,files}/*.tsx`, `lib/utils.ts`) and the CLI would either refuse the non-empty directory or overwrite them. The user explicitly confirmed the "scaffold preserving mockups" path.

Files written:
- `package.json`: added `next ^15.4.0`, `react ^19.1.0`, `react-dom ^19.1.0`, `typescript ^5.7.2`, `@types/node`, `@types/react`, `@types/react-dom`, plus scripts `dev`, `build`, `start`, `lint`, `typecheck`. Other runtime dependencies (`zustand`, `@tanstack/react-query`, AWS SDK packages, `zod`, `ulid`) are intentionally deferred to subtask 1.4.
- `tsconfig.json`: enabled `strict`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, `noImplicitOverride`, set `moduleResolution: bundler`, `jsx: preserve`, path alias `@/* -> ./*`, and the Next TypeScript plugin.
- `next.config.ts`: minimal `NextConfig` with `reactStrictMode`.
- `next-env.d.ts`: standard Next.js reference file.
- `app/layout.tsx`: minimal `RootLayout` that imports `./globals.css` so the existing design tokens remain active.
- `app/page.tsx`: placeholder landing page with a link to `/login`.

Preserved untouched: `app/globals.css`, `app/(auth)/login/page.tsx`, `components/**/*`, `lib/utils.ts`, `.env`.

Pitfalls / deferred validation:
1. `pnpm dev` will not start until subtask 1.2 adds Tailwind v4 PostCSS support (`@tailwindcss/postcss` and `postcss.config.mjs`), because the existing `globals.css` already contains `@import "tailwindcss";`.
2. `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` requires `pnpm install` first; dependency installation is bundled into subtask 1.4 to avoid duplicate install passes. The `tsconfig.json` content itself was manually verified.
3. The existing `lib/utils.ts` likely depends on `clsx` and/or `tailwind-merge` in the usual shadcn pattern; those are expected to land in subtask 1.3 with `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest init`, so imports of `@/lib/utils` may not typecheck until then.

Alternatives considered:
- Running `pnpm create next-app . --typescript --app --tailwind --eslint --import-alias "@/*"` after temporarily backing up mockups was rejected as more error-prone than writing the small scaffold files directly.
- Omitting `app/page.tsx` and relying only on `app/(auth)/login/page.tsx` was rejected because `/` would 404, while this subtask’s smoke-test expectation is that visiting `/` renders a page.

Current status: the acceptance criteria for this subtask are met with respect to project scaffold presence and strict TypeScript settings. Runtime validation (`pnpm dev`, green typecheck) remains blocked on subtask 1.2 and subtask 1.4.
</info added on 2026-05-20T11:37:41.607Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm dev` and confirm the app starts on localhost:3000 and the default `/` page renders. Run `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` to verify the strict TypeScript configuration passes without errors.

subtasks.title: Configure Tailwind CSS v4 via app/globals.css using @import and @theme
subtasks.description: Adjust the default styling setup to use Tailwind CSS v4's CSS-first configuration without any tailwind.config.js file.
subtasks.details:
Ensure there is no `tailwind.config.js` or `tailwind.config.ts` in the repository. Edit `app/globals.css` to use Tailwind v4 syntax, starting with `@import 'tailwindcss';` and define design tokens with an `@theme { ... }` block. Keep global resets and base app styles in this file as needed for the project. Do not move theme tokens into JS config files because this task requires CSS-based Tailwind v4 configuration only.
<info added on 2026-05-20T12:10:40.615Z>
Implementation verified against current Tailwind v4 docs (v4.3 line). Installed and resolved `tailwindcss` 4.3.0, `@tailwindcss/postcss` 4.3.0, and explicit `postcss` 8.5.15 per v4 install requirements. Created `postcss.config.mjs` with a single plugin entry for `@tailwindcss/postcss`; no `postcss-import` or `autoprefixer` is needed in v4 because those behaviors are folded into the Tailwind PostCSS plugin.

`app/globals.css` required no changes because it already used valid v4 syntax: `@import "tailwindcss";`, the existing `@custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *));`, and an `@theme inline { ... }` block mapping CSS variables to Tailwind tokens. Confirmed that no `tailwind.config.js` or `tailwind.config.ts` exists, preserving the required CSS-first configuration.

Verification performed: `pnpm install` succeeded, `pnpm dev` started Next.js 15.5.18 successfully, and the served compiled CSS at `/_next/static/css/app/layout.css` contained utilities used by `app/page.tsx` including `mx-auto`, `min-h-screen`, `max-w-2xl`, `flex`, and `text-primary`. Presence of `text-primary` confirms semantic token generation from the `@theme inline` mapping (`--color-primary` -> `var(--primary)`) is working end-to-end.

Expected side effect: Next.js updated `next-env.d.ts` on first dev start to include the typed-routes reference path; this is normal for Next 15. Not in scope for this subtask: the Google Fonts `@import url(...)` in `globals.css` still works but should later be migrated to `next/font/google`, and `lib/utils.ts` may still reference `clsx`/`tailwind-merge` until shadcn initialization in subtask 1.3. Acceptance criteria for this subtask are met: dev server compiles, utilities are generated from `globals.css`, and no v3-style Tailwind config file is present.
</info added on 2026-05-20T12:10:40.615Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm dev` and confirm styles compile successfully. Add a temporary Tailwind utility class to `app/page.tsx` such as `className="p-8 text-2xl"` and verify the styling is applied in the browser. Confirm no Tailwind config file exists.

subtasks.title: Initialize shadcn/ui and generate the baseline UI components into components/ui
subtasks.description: Set up shadcn/ui for the project and add the required reusable base components using the generator instead of manual edits.
subtasks.details:
Run `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest init` and configure it to place components under `components/ui/` with the project's Tailwind and App Router settings. Then generate the required components with shadcn commands, including at minimum: `button`, `input`, `dialog`, `table`, `dropdown-menu`, `toast`, `tabs`, `badge`, and `progress`. Do not handcraft files inside `components/ui/`; if a component is wrong, regenerate it through the CLI. Ensure any required support files such as `components.json` and utility helpers expected by shadcn are created correctly.
<info added on 2026-05-20T12:32:01.722Z>
Implementation note: instead of running the interactive `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest init` wizard, `components.json` was created manually to avoid overwriting the customized `app/globals.css` design tokens and the extended `lib/utils.ts` helpers that must be preserved from the mockups.

`components.json` was verified against the current shadcn v4 schema and configured as: `style: "new-york"`, `tailwind.config: ""`, `tailwind.css: "app/globals.css"`, `baseColor: "stone"`, `rsc: true`, `tsx: true`, `iconLibrary: "lucide"`, with aliases aligned to `tsconfig.json` (`@/*` → `./*`) for components, utils, ui, lib, and hooks.

Installed required runtime/peer dependencies via `pnpm add`: `clsx@2.1.1`, `tailwind-merge@3.6.0`, `class-variance-authority@0.7.1`, `lucide-react@1.16.0`, and `tw-animate-css@1.4.0`. The shadcn add flow also pulled in runtime dependencies including `sonner`, `next-themes`, and the needed `@radix-ui/react-*` packages, all kept in `dependencies` because they ship to the client.

Generated components through `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add` only, with no manual edits to primitives. Files created under `components/ui/`: `button.tsx`, `input.tsx`, `dialog.tsx`, `table.tsx`, `dropdown-menu.tsx`, `sonner.tsx`, `tabs.tsx`, `badge.tsx`, `progress.tsx`, and `label.tsx`.

Component substitution note: the original `toast` requirement is satisfied by `sonner`, which replaced the legacy shadcn Toast. `label` was also added as a needed baseline form primitive for the existing login mockup.

Verification completed:
`app/globals.css` remained unchanged after shadcn generation.
`pnpm typecheck` passed across the existing mockups and all generated components.
`pnpm dev` served `/` successfully.
Rendered Button output and compiled CSS confirmed semantic utility classes resolve correctly through the existing Tailwind v4 `@theme inline` tokens.

As a persistent smoke test, `app/page.tsx` now renders two Buttons (`default` and `outline`). This is temporary and can be replaced by dashboard routes in later tasks.

Notes for follow-up tasks: shadcn v4 components include `data-slot` attributes that can be used as stable Playwright selectors; `border-input` utilities depend on the existing `--input` token in `globals.css`; when toast notifications are wired later, mount the Sonner toaster in the root layout; monitor bundle size impact from the added Radix runtime packages if client payload becomes a concern.
</info added on 2026-05-20T12:32:01.722Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Create a temporary test render on `app/page.tsx` using the generated `Button` component and confirm it renders. Verify the expected files exist under `components/ui/` and that `pnpm lint` and `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` complete without component import errors.

subtasks.title: Install core runtime dependencies with exact pinned versions for app, validation, state, and AWS SDK
subtasks.description: Add the MVP runtime libraries and ensure security-sensitive packages are version-pinned without range prefixes.
subtasks.details:
Install the required dependencies using exact versions, for example with `pnpm add --save-exact zustand @tanstack/react-query zod ulid @aws-sdk/client-s3 @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner`. Confirm `package.json` writes exact versions with no `^` or `~` prefix, especially for `@aws-sdk/*` packages and other security-relevant runtime packages. Keep dependency additions limited to runtime libraries in this subtask; formatting and script setup belongs elsewhere.
<info added on 2026-05-20T12:33:40.735Z>
Installed the required runtime dependencies with exact pinned versions via `pnpm add --save-exact`: `@aws-sdk/client-s3@3.1050.0`, `@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner@3.1050.0`, `@tanstack/react-query@5.100.11`, `ulid@3.0.2`, `zod@4.4.3`, and `zustand@5.0.13`. Confirmed all six are written to `dependencies` without `^` or `~`, satisfying the CLAUDE.md security invariant for exact pinning of `@aws-sdk/*` and related security-relevant runtime packages. No separate crypto dependency was added because runtime crypto helpers will use the platform Web Crypto API (`crypto.subtle`).

Verified `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` completes cleanly and the lockfile matches `package.json`. Verified `pnpm typecheck` still passes under strict TypeScript settings, with no new `@types` issues since these packages ship their own types.

Compatibility notes for downstream tasks: `zod` is now v4, so older v3 patterns such as `z.string().nonempty()` must be replaced with `z.string().min(1)` and some `.default()` behavior differs; `zustand` v5 has tighter middleware typings and changed some React utility assumptions; `@tanstack/react-query` v5 requires the object-style mutation/query APIs rather than legacy v4 positional forms. For AWS SDK usage, always construct S3 clients with explicit credentials and avoid fs-based credential providers such as `fromIni`, which are not appropriate for Workers/Pages runtimes. Also keep imports command-scoped in later R2 helpers to limit bundle size impact from `@aws-sdk/client-s3`.

Acceptance for subtask 1.4 is met: all required MVP runtime libraries are installed, exact-pinned where required, lockfile is consistent, and types remain clean.
</info added on 2026-05-20T12:33:40.735Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Inspect `package.json` to verify exact version strings are present. Run `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` or a clean `pnpm install` to confirm lockfile consistency, then run `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` to ensure the added packages do not introduce type issues.

subtasks.title: Configure ESLint, Prettier, and package scripts for typecheck, lint, and formatting
subtasks.description: Finalize code quality tooling by ensuring linting, formatting, and validation scripts are available and compatible with the initialized Next.js project.
subtasks.details:
Keep the Next.js ESLint setup from scaffolding, then add Prettier integration and define package scripts in `package.json` such as `"typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"`, `"lint": "next lint"` or the Next.js 15-compatible lint command already used by the project, and `"format": "prettier --write ."`. Add any needed Prettier config file such as `.prettierrc` and optional ignore file `.prettierignore`. Ensure the script set supports routine validation for this MVP baseline.
<info added on 2026-05-20T12:41:40.787Z>
Implemented ESLint + Prettier tooling per current Next.js 15 docs using flat-config ESLint and Prettier integration. Added devDependencies with caret ranges and validated resolved versions: eslint 9.39.4, eslint-config-next 16.2.6, eslint-config-prettier 10.1.8, prettier 3.8.3.

Created `eslint.config.mjs` as ESM flat config using `eslint-config-next/core-web-vitals`, `eslint-config-next/typescript`, and `eslint-config-prettier/flat` in that order, with Prettier last to disable conflicting rules. Added global ignores for `.next/**`, `out/**`, `build/**`, `coverage/**`, `playwright-report/**`, `test-results/**`, `next-env.d.ts`, and `node_modules/**`.

Added `.prettierrc` with 80 character print width, double quotes, semicolons, trailing commas `all`, 2-space indentation, and LF line endings. Added `.prettierignore` for standard generated/build directories plus `components/ui` so shadcn-generated primitives are not hand-reformatted and can be safely regenerated by the CLI without noisy diffs.

Updated `package.json` scripts so linting uses the ESLint CLI instead of deprecated `next lint`: `lint: "eslint ."`, plus `lint:fix`, `format`, and `format:check`. `typecheck` remained in place from subtask 1.1.

Resolved an initial compatibility issue where the first install pulled `eslint@10`, which broke a transitive `eslint-plugin-react` path used by `eslint-config-next`; pinning back to `eslint@^9` aligned with the tested peer range and cleared the warnings/errors.

Fixed two pre-existing lint violations in `components/layout/app-shell.tsx`: replaced the brand `<a href="/">` with `next/link`, and changed the `useState([...])` eager initializer containing `Date.now()` to a lazy initializer `useState<Connection[]>(() => [...])` so the impure call is not executed on every render.

Established a clean formatting baseline by ignoring `components/ui` and running Prettier across the remaining files; subsequent `pnpm format:check` passes cleanly.

Validation complete: `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm lint`, and `pnpm format:check` all pass with no errors. This satisfies the subtask acceptance criteria and completes the parent project-initialization task’s lint/format tooling baseline.
</info added on 2026-05-20T12:41:40.787Z>

tasks.title: Cloudflare Pages + Edge Runtime 配置
tasks.description: 配置 wrangler.toml、next.config.ts 以支持 Cloudflare Pages 部署和 edge runtime；准备 D1 binding；所有 route handler 加 `export const runtime = 'edge'` 规范。
tasks.details: 1) 安装 `wrangler` 和 `@cloudflare/next-on-pages`；2) 创建 wrangler.toml：name、compatibility_date、compatibility_flags=['nodejs_compat']、[[d1_databases]] binding=DB、database_name、database_id；3) next.config.ts 启用 `experimental: { runtime: 'edge' }`（若适用）或确保 route handler 用 edge；4) package.json scripts: dev/build/preview(`wrangler pages dev .vercel/output/static --d1 DB=...`)/deploy(`wrangler pages deploy .vercel/output/static`)；5) 写 `pnpm build` -> `next-on-pages` 流程；6) lib 文件需要服务端时统一 `import 'server-only'`；7) 创建一个示例 `app/api/health/route.ts` 含 `export const runtime = 'edge'` 用于验证。
tasks.testStrategy: `pnpm build` 成功生成 `.vercel/output`；`pnpm preview` 本地启动 wrangler dev 后 `curl localhost:8788/api/health` 返回 200；wrangler dry-run deploy 通过。

tasks.title: D1 数据库 schema 与迁移
tasks.description: 实现 5 张表（users / connections / shares / audit_log / sessions），写迁移脚本（drizzle-kit 或纯 SQL），并提供本地与 production 的执行命令。
tasks.details: 选 drizzle-orm + drizzle-kit（edge 友好、类型安全）。1) `pnpm add drizzle-orm`，`pnpm add -D drizzle-kit @cloudflare/workers-types`；2) 在 lib/db/schema.ts 用 sqliteTable 定义 5 张表，字段照 PRD 第 3 段：users(id ulid PK, email unique, password_hash, external_id, created_at)、connections(id, user_id FK, name, account_id, endpoint, access_key_masked, access_key_ciphertext blob, access_key_iv blob, secret_key_ciphertext blob, secret_key_iv blob, created_at, last_used_at)、shares(id, user_id, connection_id, bucket, object_key, url_hash, ttl_seconds, expires_at, created_at)、audit_log(id, user_id, connection_id, op, bucket, object_key, status, error_msg, ip, ua, created_at)、sessions(id, user_id, token_hash, expires_at, created_at)；3) 索引：idx_connections_user、idx_shares_user_active(user_id, expires_at)、idx_audit_user_time(user_id, created_at DESC)；4) drizzle.config.ts 输出 SQL 到 migrations/；5) scripts: `db:gen`、`db:migrate:local`（wrangler d1 migrations apply DB --local）、`db:migrate:prod`；6) lib/db/client.ts 工厂：`export function getDb(env: Env) { return drizzle(env.DB, { schema }); }`。
tasks.testStrategy: `pnpm db:gen` 生成 SQL；`pnpm db:migrate:local` 成功在本地 D1 创建所有表；vitest 单测：在 in-memory better-sqlite3 上跑迁移，校验表/索引存在；插入/读取一行 users 行成功。

subtasks.title: Define the five D1 sqliteTable schemas in lib/db/schema.ts
subtasks.description: Create the Drizzle schema file and implement users, connections, shares, audit_log, and sessions tables exactly matching the PRD field requirements.
subtasks.details:
Use sqliteTable and appropriate Drizzle SQLite column types to model all required fields: users(id ulid PK, email unique, password_hash, external_id, created_at), connections(id, user_id FK, name, account_id, endpoint, access_key_masked, access_key_ciphertext blob, access_key_iv blob, secret_key_ciphertext blob, secret_key_iv blob, created_at, last_used_at), shares(id, user_id, connection_id, bucket, object_key, url_hash, ttl_seconds, expires_at, created_at), audit_log(id, user_id, connection_id, op, bucket, object_key, status, error_msg, ip, ua, created_at), and sessions(id, user_id, token_hash, expires_at, created_at). Preserve naming compatibility with D1/SQLite and export the schema members for downstream use.
<info added on 2026-05-20T13:14:06.552Z>
Implemented lib/db/schema.ts with all five PRD tables using Drizzle’s sqliteTable API. Chosen convention is camelCase TypeScript property names with explicit snake_case SQL column names on every field to preserve the PRD schema exactly while keeping idiomatic JS access.

Column/type decisions:
- All primary keys use text("id").primaryKey() and store ULID strings; ULIDs are generated in application code at insert time via the ulid package rather than a DB default.
- Timestamp fields use integer({ mode: "timestamp" }); createdAt uses $defaultFn(() => new Date()) so callers do not need to pass it explicitly. Nullable vs required timestamp columns follow the PRD semantics, including nullable lastUsedAt and required expiresAt where applicable.
- Secret/cipher fields use blob({ mode: "buffer" }) for access_key_ciphertext, access_key_iv, secret_key_ciphertext, and secret_key_iv to support both Node-based tooling/tests and Workers runtime with nodejs_compat enabled.
- users.email is defined as notNull + unique, relying on SQLite’s implicit unique index.

Foreign keys were implemented inline with .references(..., { onDelete }) in this file:

tasks.description: 实现 lib/crypto/aes-gcm.ts：基于 Web Crypto subtle，AES-GCM-256，每行随机 12 字节 IV，AAD 用 connection.id；导出 encryptCredential / decryptCredential，主密钥从 env ENCRYPTION_KEY (base64, 32 字节) 加载。
tasks.details: 1) 文件首行 `import 'server-only'`；2) `async function importKey(rawBase64: string): Promise<CryptoKey>` 用 `crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', bytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['encrypt','decrypt'])`，缓存在模块级 WeakMap；3) `async function encryptCredential(plaintext: string, aad: string, env): Promise<{iv: Uint8Array, ciphertext: Uint8Array}>`：随机 IV via `crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(12))`，调用 `subtle.encrypt({ name: 'AES-GCM', iv, additionalData: te.encode(aad) }, key, te.encode(plaintext))`；4) `async function decryptCredential(ciphertext, iv, aad, env): Promise<string>`：tag 校验失败必须抛 `CryptoIntegrityError`（自定义类）；5) 不做密钥派生，主密钥直接来自 env；6) 校验 env ENCRYPTION_KEY 启动时长度 = 32 字节，否则抛错；7) 不写明文凭证到任何日志。
tasks.testStrategy: Vitest 单测：round-trip（加密+解密相等）；不同 IV 对同一 plaintext 产出不同 ciphertext；篡改 1 字节 ciphertext 必抛 CryptoIntegrityError；篡改 AAD 必抛错；plaintext 含 unicode/空字符串均正确。

subtasks.title: Set up lib/crypto/aes-gcm.ts with server-only import, env key validation, and CryptoIntegrityError
subtasks.description: Create the AES-GCM module scaffold in lib/crypto/aes-gcm.ts, add startup-safe key decoding and length validation for ENCRYPTION_KEY, and define the custom CryptoIntegrityError class.
subtasks.details:
Implement lib/crypto/aes-gcm.ts with `import 'server-only'` at the top, shared TextEncoder/TextDecoder utilities, base64 decoding of `env.ENCRYPTION_KEY`, and a validation path that ensures the decoded key is exactly 32 bytes for AES-256-GCM. Add a custom `CryptoIntegrityError` class for authentication/tag failures. Ensure validation throws a clear error when the env key is missing, malformed base64, or not 32 bytes. Do not log plaintext credentials or raw key material anywhere.
<info added on 2026-05-20T14:11:35.809Z>
Implemented in lib/crypto/aes-gcm.ts:1-89.

Design decisions:
- validateAndDecodeKey must throw synchronously before any cache write, preventing an invalid key from storing a rejected Promise in the module cache.
- Define CryptoEnv with only ENCRYPTION_KEY to avoid overlapping with DbEnv; compose the full Env shape at the route/service layer instead.
- Set CryptoIntegrityError.name explicitly to "CryptoIntegrityError" rather than relying only on Error inheritance, so callers can distinguish integrity/authentication failures from configuration errors for different handling paths (for example 401/audit event vs 5xx).

Pitfalls and implementation notes:
- The original task mentions a module-level WeakMap cache, but WeakMap keys must be objects, so a base64 string key is invalid. Use Map<string, Promise<CryptoKey>> instead, and cache the Promise itself so concurrent first callers share the same importKey operation.
- With combined lib typings (dom + webworker + @cloudflare/workers-types), BufferSource constraints become stricter. Explicitly type decodeBase64 and validateAndDecodeKey return values as Uint8Array<ArrayBuffer>; otherwise downstream crypto.subtle.importKey may be rejected by TypeScript 5.7 because SharedArrayBuffer-backed views do not satisfy the expected ArrayBufferView constraints.
</info added on 2026-05-20T14:11:35.809Z>

subtasks.title: Implement importKey(rawBase64) with Web Crypto import and module-level cache
subtasks.description: Add the internal importKey helper that converts the validated base64 master key into a reusable CryptoKey and caches it at module scope.
subtasks.details:
In lib/crypto/aes-gcm.ts, implement `async function importKey(rawBase64: string): Promise<CryptoKey>` using `crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', bytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['encrypt','decrypt'])`. Add a module-level cache so repeated calls with the same base64 key reuse the same imported CryptoKey instead of re-importing. The cache should be internal to the module and should not expose key bytes. Keep the implementation compatible with Web Crypto in the Node/Vitest runtime used by the project.
<info added on 2026-05-20T14:11:40.952Z>
Implement getMasterKey and keyCache in lib/crypto/aes-gcm.ts around lines 62-89. Use a module-level Map keyed by the raw base64 string, with values as Promise<CryptoKey> rather than resolved CryptoKey instances so concurrent first-time callers await the same crypto.subtle.importKey operation and do not duplicate imports. In getMasterKey/import flow, call validateAndDecodeKey first so invalid env input throws synchronously before touching the cache; this guarantees the cache never stores a rejected Promise caused by bad key material. After creating the import promise, attach promise.catch(() => keyCache.delete(rawBase64)) so any unexpected async import failure clears the poisoned entry and allows later retries. Keep importKey extractable=false so the imported AES key cannot be read back via subtle.exportKey. For TS 5.7+ compatibility with BufferSource, ensure decodeBase64 returns Uint8Array<ArrayBuffer>, and in validateAndDecodeKey declare let bytes: Uint8Array<ArrayBuffer>; avoid widening back to plain Uint8Array<ArrayBufferLike>, which can cause crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', ...) type rejection.
</info added on 2026-05-20T14:11:40.952Z>

subtasks.title: Implement encryptCredential with random 12-byte IV and AAD binding
subtasks.description: Build the public encryptCredential function to encrypt a plaintext credential using AES-GCM-256, a fresh random IV per call, and caller-provided AAD.
subtasks.details:
In lib/crypto/aes-gcm.ts, implement `encryptCredential(plaintext: string, aad: string, env): Promise<{ iv: Uint8Array, ciphertext: Uint8Array }>` using `crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(12))` for the IV and `crypto.subtle.encrypt` with `{ name: 'AES-GCM', iv, additionalData: te.encode(aad) }`. Encode plaintext and AAD with TextEncoder, import the master key from `env.ENCRYPTION_KEY`, and return Uint8Array views for both IV and ciphertext. Preserve support for empty strings and Unicode plaintext. Do not emit any logs containing plaintext.
<info added on 2026-05-20T14:11:45.735Z>
Implement at lib/crypto/aes-gcm.ts:104-118 with these constraints and rationale:
Use a fresh IV on every call via crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(12)); IV reuse with the same key is forbidden for GCM because it enables plaintext xor leakage and tag forgery.
Return separate { iv, ciphertext } fields instead of concatenating them, to match the existing D1 schema blobs such as access_key_iv and access_key_ciphertext and avoid storage/schema mismatch.
Keep AAD fully caller-supplied through the aad parameter rather than hardcoding connection.id so the same helper can be reused by other tables/usages such as shares.
Do not manually split or extract the authentication tag; Web Crypto appends the 16-byte GCM tag to the end of ciphertext automatically.
TypeScript note: te.encode(plaintext) is already accepted by subtle.encrypt under the current TS lib typings, so no extra BufferSource cast is needed here; the explicit cast concern only applies on decryptCredential inputs read back from D1 blobs.
</info added on 2026-05-20T14:11:45.735Z>

subtasks.title: Implement decryptCredential with integrity error mapping for tampering or AAD mismatch

In lib/crypto/aes-gcm.ts, implement `decryptCredential(ciphertext, iv, aad, env): Promise<string>` using `crypto.subtle.decrypt` with the supplied IV and `additionalData` derived from the AAD string. Decode the resulting plaintext with TextDecoder. Catch Web Crypto decryption/tag verification failures and rethrow them specifically as `CryptoIntegrityError`, while preserving unexpected configuration or environment errors as normal exceptions. Ensure the function works with Uint8Array inputs and does not log decrypted plaintext or ciphertext content.
<info added on 2026-05-20T14:11:50.838Z>
Implementation note for lib/crypto/aes-gcm.ts:127-149: call getMasterKey(env) outside the try/catch, and wrap only crypto.subtle.decrypt so ENCRYPTION_KEY/config/importKey failures bubble as plain Error for route-level 5xx mapping. Accept iv and ciphertext as Uint8Array-compatible inputs and cast internally to BufferSource for subtle.decrypt to avoid TypeScript friction with D1 Blob-derived Buffer/Uint8Array<ArrayBufferLike> values. Avoid the anti-pattern of catching and remapping all errors, which would incorrectly convert env/importKey failures into integrity errors. Test expectations: tampered ciphertext, mismatched AAD, wrong IV, and wrong master key must all throw CryptoIntegrityError, while env/config failures must explicitly not throw CryptoIntegrityError.

subtasks.title: Implement Edge-safe PBKDF2 password utilities in lib/auth/password.ts
subtasks.description: Create password hashing and verification helpers using Web Crypto only, with the required PBKDF2-SHA256 format and constant-time comparison.
subtasks.details:
Add hashPassword(plain) and verifyPassword(plain, stored) in lib/auth/password.ts using crypto.subtle and crypto.getRandomValues only. Use 600000 iterations, 16-byte random salt, SHA-256, and serialize as pbkdf2$sha256$600000$<salt-b64>$<hash-b64>. Parse and validate the stored format during verification, recompute the derived key, and compare byte arrays with a constant-time equality function. Do not import or depend on Node crypto APIs so the module remains Edge-compatible.
<info added on 2026-05-20T14:39:20.224Z>
Implementation notes for lib/auth/password.ts and tests/unit/auth/password.test.ts:
Use PBKDF2-SHA256 with 600000 iterations, 16-byte random salt, and a 32-byte derived key. Keep the serialized storage format as a single field: pbkdf2$sha256$600000$<salt-b64>$<hash-b64>, so algorithm/iteration upgrades remain versionable via the prefix.
Implement constant-time comparison as: return false immediately on differing lengths, otherwise XOR-accumulate all bytes and check diff === 0 to avoid byte-by-byte short-circuit leakage.
Behavior rules: hashPassword must explicitly throw on empty string input; verifyPassword must return false for any malformed input or parse failure instead of throwing, so reads do not distinguish nonexistent user / bad format / wrong password.
Do not import server-only in this module. The original note caused scripts/seed-admin.ts running under tsx require to synchronously throw from server-only. This file is pure computation with no env secret access, so Edge-safe Web Crypto only is sufficient; add a file header comment explaining why server-only is intentionally omitted. Keep server-only only in secret-bearing modules such as lib/crypto/aes-gcm.ts.
Account for the TS 5.7 Uint8Array<ArrayBuffer> narrowing issue by explicitly annotating the relevant Uint8Array generics in helpers such as fromBase64 and deriveKey.
Acceptance: all 6 Vitest cases in tests/unit/auth/password.test.ts must pass.
</info added on 2026-05-20T14:39:20.224Z>

subtasks.title: Build a minimal D1 Auth.js adapter in lib/auth/adapter.ts with hashed session tokens
subtasks.description: Implement the smallest custom adapter needed for Auth.js database sessions backed by D1, storing only SHA-256 hashes of session tokens.
subtasks.details:
Create a compact D1 adapter in lib/auth/adapter.ts implementing getUser, createSession, getSessionAndUser, and deleteSession. Accept a D1 database binding and query the existing users and sessions tables. Before persisting a session, hash the raw token with Web Crypto SHA-256 and store it in sessions.token_hash instead of storing the original token. When reading or deleting sessions, hash the incoming token first, then match by token_hash. Keep the implementation concise, typed, and Edge-safe.
<info added on 2026-05-20T14:39:30.363Z>
Implement createD1Adapter(db) as a small helper used manually from Auth.js callbacks, not as an @auth/core Adapter. Keep Auth.js on JWT session strategy, and expose Auth.js-like methods only to minimize future migration cost: getUser, createSession, getSessionAndUser, deleteSession. Also export getUserWithPassword separately for authorize(), because it must return passwordHash while normal user lookup paths must not expose that field.

Persist sessions by hashing the raw ULID token with Web Crypto SHA-256 and storing the 64-char lowercase hex digest in sessions.token_hash. Never store the raw token in D1; it should exist only inside the signed JWT. Use hex instead of base64 because token_hash is a plain TEXT column and hex is simpler for indexing and equality lookups.

Because the current Drizzle schema does not declare relations(...), do not use .findFirst({ with: { user: true } }). Implement getSessionAndUser as two queries: first load the session by token_hash and validate it, then fetch the user by session.userId. This keeps the short-circuit behavior of only loading the user when the session is valid, with negligible performance impact here.

Make deleteSession idempotent: repeated deletes during concurrent refresh/signOut flows should succeed silently without throwing if the row is already gone.
</info added on 2026-05-20T14:39:30.363Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Use adapter-focused unit tests or integration-style tests with a mock/in-memory DB to verify session creation, lookup by hashed token, joined session+user retrieval, and deletion behavior.

subtasks.title: Configure Auth.js v5 credentials flow in lib/auth/config.ts
subtasks.description: Set up the Auth.js configuration for self-hosted credentials login, database-backed sessions, and secure cookie behavior.
subtasks.details:
Create lib/auth/config.ts exporting the Auth.js configuration with CredentialsProvider. In authorize(), load the single user by email from D1, verify the submitted password through verifyPassword(), and return the user object on success or null on failure. Configure session.strategy = 'database', wire in the custom D1 adapter, and define cookies with sameSite: 'lax' and secure enabled in secure environments. Ensure the config is compatible with Edge runtime expectations and does not rely on Node-only APIs.
<info added on 2026-05-20T14:39:41.694Z>
Adopt Auth.js v5 split-config structure. In lib/auth/config.ts, keep an edge-safe base config only: pages, trustHost, session strategy, authorized callback, and PUBLIC_PATHS allowlist, with no DB, adapter, or provider imports so middleware can consume it safely on Edge. Use PUBLIC_PATHS = "/", "/login", "/api/auth", "/api/health"; all other paths must require a valid session and fail closed by default.

Change session.strategy to "jwt" with maxAge = 7 days. The authorized callback should treat missing/expired/revoked sessions as unauthenticated and redirect protected requests to /login.

Move the full NextAuth assembly to lib/auth/index.ts by extending the base config with the Credentials provider, custom D1 adapter, and jwt/session/signOut callbacks. In Credentials authorize(), fetch the user row by email including passwordHash, verify via verifyPassword(), and return the user on success or null on failure; never throw for invalid credentials so Auth.js uses the standard CredentialsSignin path.

Implement the known Auth.js v5 workaround for Credentials + database-style session semantics: keep JWT strategy, but on jwt() callback create a D1 session row manually through adapter.createSession only when trigger === "signIn" and user is present. Reuse the existing session token on later jwt refreshes to avoid duplicate INSERTs. Keep D1 sessions.expires_at aligned with the 7-day JWT maxAge so deleting the D1 row acts as revocation on the next auth() check.

In session() callback, look up the backing D1 session row to confirm it still exists and is not expired. If the row is missing or invalid, return the session in an unauthenticated shape by clearing user and forcing expires to the epoch (for example user: undefined as never and expires = new Date(0).toISOString()), so authorized() will reject the next protected request. Add signOut handling to delete the corresponding D1 session row on logout.
</info added on 2026-05-20T14:39:41.694Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Add unit-level checks around authorize() for valid and invalid credentials, and verify the exported config contains database session strategy and expected cookie options.

subtasks.title: Expose Auth.js route handlers in app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts
subtasks.description: Create the App Router auth endpoint that exports the Auth.js handlers using the shared configuration.
subtasks.details:
Add app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts and initialize Auth.js v5 with the config from lib/auth/config.ts. Export the generated GET and POST handlers required by Next.js App Router. Mark or structure the file so it is safe for Edge deployment if the rest of the stack is Edge-compatible. Ensure the route is the single entry point used by the login form submission and sign-out/session operations.
<info added on 2026-05-20T14:39:47.811Z>
Refactor the auth endpoint into three layers: app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts should stay minimal with export const runtime = "edge" and a re-export of GET/POST from lib/auth/handlers.ts; lib/auth/handlers.ts should import handlers from lib/auth/index.ts, destructure { GET, POST }, and expose them for the App Router. This keeps route.ts to three lines and isolates future Auth.js API changes to handlers.ts.

Use runtime = "edge" on the route because the full auth config depends on getRequestContext() to access the D1 binding, which is only available in the Pages worker context.

Typing note for the auth stack: do not use module augmentation for next-auth/jwt or @auth/core/jwt, as both can fail with "Invalid module name in augmentation" under jose v6 / next-auth v5 beta with moduleResolution: "bundler" and module: "esnext". Instead, define a local SessionJWT interface in lib/auth/index.ts and use a one-time callback-local assertion such as token as typeof token & SessionJWT where needed. Session augmentation for declare module "next-auth" remains acceptable because next-auth is explicitly imported.

Do not add route-level comments about the jose v6 DecompressionStream edge-runtime warning; that dead-code warning is documented next to SessionJWT in lib/auth/index.ts.
</info added on 2026-05-20T14:39:47.811Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Manually verify that /api/auth/* routes resolve without server errors, and add a smoke test that imports the route module and confirms GET/POST handlers are defined.

subtasks.title: Add protected route middleware for dashboard redirects in middleware.ts
subtasks.description: Implement middleware that checks auth state for dashboard paths and redirects unauthenticated users to the login page.
subtasks.details:
Create or update middleware.ts to intercept requests targeting /(dashboard) paths, such as the actual dashboard URL namespace used by the app. Use Auth.js session/auth helpers that work in middleware/Edge context to detect whether a valid session exists. Redirect unauthenticated requests to /login, ideally preserving a callback URL if helpful, and allow authenticated requests through. Keep matcher scope narrow so static assets and public routes are unaffected.
<info added on 2026-05-20T14:39:54.599Z>
middleware.ts should live at the project root and export Auth.js middleware directly via export default NextAuth(authConfig).auth, reusing the edge-safe split config from lib/auth/config.ts only. Do not import the adapter, D1 access, or anything requiring getRequestContext, because Next.js middleware runs in an isolated Edge runtime and any DB call will fail there.

Move the actual protected-route decision into authConfig.authorized, using a PUBLIC_PATHS allowlist there. The middleware matcher should only do coarse filtering so Auth.js is not invoked for obvious static/public asset requests.

Use a negative-lookahead matcher like /((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|.*\.(?:png|jpg|jpeg|svg|ico|webp|css|js|map)).*) to skip Next internals and static assets. Excluding .js and .map is important to avoid intercepting Next.js dev client bundle requests.

Do not add D1/session freshness checks to middleware even if Cloudflare Pages middleware can see env in the worker: it would add a D1 round-trip to every protected page request, effectively doubling QPS for protected navigation, and it breaks the split-config boundary between Edge-safe auth gating and DB-backed session validation. Any stronger real-time validation should remain in the session() callback running in route handlers, where DB access already exists.

Bundle size note: the middleware bundle is about 85.5 kB including next-auth + jose, which is comfortably within the Cloudflare Pages worker 1 MB limit.
</info added on 2026-05-20T14:39:54.599Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Add middleware behavior tests where possible, and manually verify that visiting a protected page while logged out redirects to /login while logged-in access succeeds.

subtasks.title: Build the login page UI in app/(auth)/login/page.tsx
subtasks.description: Create a login screen with email/password inputs that submits to the Auth.js credentials endpoint and shows failure states cleanly.
subtasks.details:
Implement app/(auth)/login/page.tsx as a client component or server-action-backed page according to project conventions. Render a minimal form for email and password, post to /api/auth/[...nextauth] using the credentials flow, and handle callback/redirect behavior after successful authentication. Include clear validation/error messaging for invalid credentials and align the page with the app's existing UI components. No registration link or signup flow should be included because admin creation is seed-only.
<info added on 2026-05-20T14:40:00.875Z>
Keep all existing UI chrome and local form state, and replace only the submit path with next-auth/react signIn("credentials", { email, password, redirect: false }). Do not switch to a server action.

Read callbackUrl from useSearchParams and, after a successful sign-in, navigate with router.replace(callbackUrl) followed by router.refresh() so the user cannot go back to /login and server-rendered auth state is re-evaluated immediately.

Normalize all credentials failures to a single client error key: setError("auth.invalid_credentials"). Do not distinguish "user not found" vs "wrong password"; Auth.js v5 collapses credential failures to CredentialsSignin anyway, and exposing distinctions on the client would enable user-enumeration behavior. Use dotted i18n placeholder keys such as "auth.invalid_credentials" instead of literal Chinese/English copy so next-intl can be wired in later without revisiting this page.

Avoid the Next.js build/prerender trap caused by calling useSearchParams() at the top level of a client page. Split the file into a default-export LoginPage wrapper that renders a Suspense boundary and an inner LoginForm component that uses useSearchParams. This preserves /login as a static route while allowing the callbackUrl-dependent logic to hydrate client-side without triggering a prerender error or forcing the whole page to bail out to CSR.
</info added on 2026-05-20T14:40:00.875Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Manual browser verification: wrong credentials show an error, valid credentials create a session and redirect to the intended protected page or default dashboard route.

subtasks.title: Add admin seed CLI, password tests, and implementation documentation
subtasks.description: Provide the one-time admin creation script, password utility tests, and concise docs covering setup and acceptance criteria for the auth flow.
subtasks.details:
Create scripts/seed-admin.ts to read ADMIN_EMAIL and ADMIN_PASSWORD from environment, hash the password with hashPassword(), insert the admin user into users, and optionally update .dev.vars or document the required variables. Add Vitest tests focused on lib/auth/password.ts for round-trip verification, wrong-password failure, and salt uniqueness. Document implementation locations, Edge/Web Crypto constraints, how to run the seed script, and acceptance criteria for login, logout, and protected-route checks.
<info added on 2026-05-20T14:40:12.512Z>
Implemented scripts/seed-admin.ts as a pure SQL generator instead of shelling out to wrangler. The script reads ADMIN_EMAIL and ADMIN_PASSWORD from env, validates email with a narrow regex, rejects passwords shorter than 12 chars, generates a ULID in-script to match schema defaults, hashes with hashPassword(), and prints the INSERT INTO users(...) SQL to stdout while sending progress/status text to stderr so output can be piped cleanly into tee or a file. This avoids shell escaping issues with PBKDF2 hashes containing $ across pnpm script -> child_process -> wrangler layers. Expected usage is to capture the SQL and then run wrangler d1 execute prisim-r2-db --local --command "..." or --file=/tmp/seed.sql manually.

Added tests/unit/auth/password.test.ts with 6 real PBKDF2 cases and no crypto mocks: round-trip success, wrong-password failure, same plaintext producing different hashes via different salts, stored format matching the expected regex, five malformed stored-value variants all returning false, and empty plaintext being rejected. Tests intentionally run the full 600k-iteration PBKDF2 to verify the actual OWASP-strength configuration end to end; current runtime is acceptable at roughly 3 hash operations plus verifies (~800ms total).

Used file header comments as the primary documentation surface. Documented implementation locations, Edge/Web Crypto constraints, seed script behavior, why wrangler is not invoked automatically, required env vars, and auth acceptance criteria there instead of expanding separate docs.

Known follow-ups and environment caveats: AUTH_SECRET is required at runtime and should be placed in .dev.vars as AUTH_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>; this task does not create or modify .dev.vars automatically to avoid accidental commits. In next dev, getRequestContext() is unavailable unless next.config.ts enables setupDevPlatform() from @cloudflare/next-on-pages/next-dev for development; this subtask does not change next.config.ts. Seed script was manually verified via npx tsx with ADMIN_EMAIL=admin@example.com ADMIN_PASSWORD=changemenowabcd and produced a valid INSERT statement.

Testing scope note: no separate adapter unit test was added because setting up a duplicate better-sqlite3 + drizzle-orm/better-sqlite3 harness would overlap heavily with lib/db/migration.test.ts. Adapter behavior is currently intended to be covered by Auth.js callback/e2e flow, with dedicated adapter unit tests deferred unless adapter logic starts changing frequently.

Validation snapshot: pnpm test passing with 3 files / 25 tests, pnpm typecheck clean, pnpm lint clean, pnpm build successful; route output still shows /login as static and /api/auth/* plus /api/health as dynamic edge routes.
</info added on 2026-05-20T14:40:12.512Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Run the seed script against local D1 and confirm the admin row is created, execute Vitest password tests successfully, and verify the documented setup reproduces a working login flow end-to-end.

tasks.title: CSRF 保护 + 输入验证中间件
tasks.description: 为所有 POST/PATCH/DELETE 路由强制 X-CSRF-Token 校验；CSRF token 登录时随 session 下发，存 cookie + meta；统一 Zod 输入验证；统一错误响应格式。
tasks.details: 1) lib/auth/csrf.ts：登录成功时生成 32 字节随机 token，sha256(token) 与 session_id 绑定存 D1（或加列到 sessions），同时下发 httpOnly=false 的 `csrf` cookie；2) 客户端从 cookie 读后放 X-CSRF-Token header；3) lib/api/middleware.ts：`requireSession(req)` 返回 user 或 401；`requireCsrf(req, session)` 比对 hash 一致；`withApi(handler)` 高阶函数把这两步组合 + try/catch 统一错误响应；4) 统一错误：`{ error: { code, message, requestId } }`，错误码字符串集中在 lib/api/errors.ts；5) lib/api/schemas.ts 集中所有 Zod schema（z.infer 复用类型）；6) 在每个 route handler 第一行 `const input = Schema.parse(await req.json())`；7) requestId 用 crypto.randomUUID()，落审计日志。
tasks.testStrategy: Vitest 单测：POST 不带 X-CSRF-Token 返回 401；token 错误返回 401；Zod 校验失败返回 400 + code='validation.invalid'；所有错误响应都含 requestId 字段。

subtasks.title: Define unified API error codes and response formatter
subtasks.description: Create centralized error definitions and a shared formatter for consistent API error responses with request IDs.
subtasks.details:
Add lib/api/errors.ts to declare string error codes such as auth.unauthorized, csrf.invalid, validation.invalid, and internal.unexpected. Implement helpers/classes that serialize errors as { error: { code, message, requestId } } and support mapping Zod and auth failures to HTTP status codes.
<info added on 2026-05-20T15:10:29.350Z>
Implement lib/api/errors.ts with ApiErrorCode defined as an as const object and derive the ApiErrorCode union via typeof ApiErrorCode[keyof typeof ApiErrorCode] so codes are grep-friendly, runtime-enumerable, and consistently named as <domain>.<reason>. Include at least auth.unauthorized, csrf.invalid, validation.invalid, resource.not_found, confirmation.required, rate_limited, and internal.unexpected.

Add an ApiError class carrying code, status, message, and optional details, plus an ApiErrors factory that centralizes the standard code/status pairs to avoid handwritten status codes drifting at call sites.

Implement toErrorResponse to normalize thrown values into the shared payload shape { error: { code, message, requestId } } and always attach the same requestId to both the response body and x-request-id header. It must explicitly intercept ZodError from Schema.parse(...) and convert it to validation.invalid instead of leaking to a generic 500.

Unknown thrown values must always collapse to HTTP 500 with code internal.unexpected and the fixed message "Unexpected server error". Do not reuse raw Error.message in this fallback path to avoid leaking internal SQL, filesystem paths, or other server details.

Add unit coverage in tests/unit/api/errors.test.ts with 11 cases covering code-to-status mapping, ApiErrors factory helpers, ZodError normalization, unknown/internal error fallback non-leakage, and the guarantee that every error response includes requestId in both body and x-request-id header.
</info added on 2026-05-20T15:10:29.350Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test error formatter output shape, status mapping, and presence of requestId for known error types.

subtasks.title: Implement CSRF token generation and session binding on login
subtasks.description: Add CSRF token creation, hashing, persistence, and cookie issuance during successful login.
subtasks.details:
Create lib/auth/csrf.ts with logic to generate a 32-byte random token, hash it with sha256, and bind the hash to the session_id in D1 or the sessions table. Update the login flow to set a non-httpOnly csrf cookie and expose the token for client-side header usage, while never storing the raw token server-side.
<info added on 2026-05-20T15:10:41.585Z>
Adopt double-submit cookie plus server-side hash binding: issue a non-httpOnly csrf cookie readable by frontend JS, require clients to copy it into X-CSRF-Token, and validate on server with sha256(headerToken) against sessions.csrf_token_hash. Also embed the raw CSRF token in SessionJWT.csrfToken so a future /api/csrf endpoint can re-issue the cookie without an extra DB read. Session revocation remains implicit: deleting the D1 session row invalidates CSRF because requireSession must pass before CSRF validation.

Implement lib/auth/csrf.ts with generateCsrfToken() using 32 random bytes from crypto.getRandomValues encoded as base64url without padding, hashCsrfToken() producing a 64-character lowercase sha256 hex string, a timingSafeEqual() helper that returns false on length mismatch and otherwise compares via XOR aggregation, and buildCsrfCookie() that emits Path=/, Max-Age=7d, SameSite=Lax, Secure in production only, and never HttpOnly.

Schema update: add nullable csrf_token_hash text to sessions for backward compatibility with existing rows, but require all new createSession writes to populate it. Generate the migration with drizzle-kit, rename it to 0001_session_csrf_token_hash.sql, and update meta/_journal.json tag accordingly.

Adapter changes: createSession must generate both session token and csrf token, persist the session token hash plus csrf_token_hash in a single D1 write, and return { token, csrfToken }. Extend getSessionAndUser to include csrfTokenHash for middleware validation and update the SessionRecord interface with the new field.

Auth integration: in auth/index.ts, set SessionJWT.csrfToken inside the jwt() callback on signIn trigger. Because Auth.js v5 with Credentials uses JWT strategy and does not allow setting cookies from session(), rely on a GET /api/csrf bootstrap path to distribute the csrf cookie when needed.

Documented decisions: do not store raw csrf_token in D1; store only the hash for defense in depth in case the database leaks. Use SameSite=Lax instead of Strict so the cookie is available on the first post-login navigation to dashboard.

Add unit tests in tests/unit/auth/csrf.test.ts covering 11 cases including token entropy/format, deterministic hashing, timingSafeEqual length mismatch returning false without throwing, and cookie string assembly.
</info added on 2026-05-20T15:10:41.585Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test token generation length, hash consistency, persistence behavior, and login response/cookie emission.

subtasks.title: Build session, CSRF, and request wrapper middleware
subtasks.description: Implement reusable middleware helpers that enforce session auth, CSRF checks, and unified try/catch behavior.
subtasks.details:
Add lib/api/middleware.ts with requireSession(req) to resolve the current session user or throw 401, requireCsrf(req, session) to compare X-CSRF-Token hash against the stored session-bound hash, and withApi(handler) to generate requestId via crypto.randomUUID(), run auth/CSRF checks for POST/PATCH/DELETE, catch exceptions, and emit normalized error responses while writing audit logs.
<info added on 2026-05-20T15:10:53.610Z>
Implement lib/api/middleware.ts with three exports: requireSession, requireCsrf, and withApi. withApi(handler) should be a HOF used as export const POST = withApi(async (req, ctx) => { ... }), with pipeline order: generate requestId via crypto.randomUUID() -> requireSession using JWT + D1 revocation check -> for mutating methods requireCsrf -> invoke handler -> normalize success response -> catch errors with toErrorResponse. Define MUTATING_METHODS as POST/PATCH/PUT/DELETE; skip CSRF for GET/HEAD/OPTIONS.

requireSession must read the session JWT via next-auth/jwt getToken(req, secret) instead of auth() to avoid side effects, sourcing AUTH_SECRET from getRequestContext().env.AUTH_SECRET with fallback to process.env.AUTH_SECRET for local dev. Do not pass cookieName; Auth.js v5 will resolve __Secure- prefixed production cookies vs dev cookies automatically based on req.url. After decoding the JWT, perform a D1-backed revocation check by calling adapter.getSessionAndUser so that deleted session rows immediately return 401 even if the JWT is still unexpired.

requireCsrf must validate X-CSRF-Token by hashing the presented raw token and comparing it against the session-bound hash stored in D1, rather than comparing raw values directly. Missing or mismatched token should return 401 csrf.invalid.

withApi should pass a context object containing at least userId and requestId into the wrapped handler. If the handler returns any JSON-serializable value, automatically respond with Response.json(value, { status: 200 }) and include x-request-id. If the handler returns a fully formed Response, mutate its headers in place to add x-request-id only when the header is absent, preserving any custom value already set by the handler and avoiding a new Response allocation.

Error handling should map ZodError to 400 validation.invalid and suppress console.error for those cases. For non-Zod errors, log console.error with method, url, and requestId for correlation, and return a sanitized 500 response that does not leak internal details such as SQL errors. Middleware should not write audit logs; route handlers remain responsible for calling recordAudit because operation metadata is route-specific.

Add tests in tests/unit/api/middleware.test.ts with 13 cases, mocking getToken, @cloudflare/next-on-pages getRequestContext, and the auth adapter. Cover: POST without JWT -> 401 auth.unauthorized; POST missing X-CSRF-Token -> 401 csrf.invalid; POST wrong CSRF token -> 401 csrf.invalid; POST valid token -> 200 with ctx.userId and requestId available; GET skips CSRF; revoked D1 session -> 401; handler throwing ZodError -> 400 validation.invalid; handler throwing unknown Error -> 500 with sanitized body; handler returning Response -> inject x-request-id while preserving existing custom headers.
</info added on 2026-05-20T15:10:53.610Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Vitest unit tests for missing session, missing CSRF header, invalid token, valid token pass-through, and requestId generation on thrown errors.

subtasks.title: Centralize Zod schemas and inferred API input types
subtasks.description: Create a shared schema module for API payload validation and reusable TypeScript inference.
subtasks.details:
Add lib/api/schemas.ts and move or define all route input schemas there using Zod. Export both schema objects and z.infer-derived types so handlers and UI code can reuse the same contracts. Establish a naming convention per route/action to simplify imports and future expansion.
<info added on 2026-05-20T15:11:02.615Z>
Implement lib/api/schemas.ts as the single shared Zod contract module for server and client, using the naming convention <Domain><Verb>Schema plus matching exported <Domain><Verb>Input = z.infer<typeof ...>. Add shared primitive schemas: UlidSchema, BucketNameSchema, ObjectKeySchema, ConfirmationTokenSchema, and LoginSchema. BucketNameSchema should enforce S3-style bucket naming with total length 3-63 and regex ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9.-]{1,61}[a-z0-9]$ so names stay lowercase alphanumeric with . and -, and never start or end with - or .. ObjectKeySchema should require length 1-1024 and reject leading /. ConfirmationTokenSchema should enforce length 16-128. Leave commented placeholder business schemas such as ConnectionsCreateSchema so task 7 CRUD work can uncomment and extend in place.

Add a shared parseJson(req, schema) helper that combines await req.json() with schema.parse(). If JSON parsing throws SyntaxError, wrap it into a ZodError so withApi can consistently map it to 400 validation.invalid instead of leaking a raw SyntaxError as 500. When constructing the wrapped ZodError for Zod 4, include input: undefined on the issue object to satisfy the updated issue shape.

Note: Zod 4 email validation is stricter than Zod 3, so LoginSchema tests should use addresses like alice@example.com rather than a@b.c.

Add tests in tests/unit/api/schemas.test.ts covering 13 cases total: boundary and invalid/valid cases for each primitive schema, plus parseJson with valid JSON, schema-mismatched JSON, and malformed non-JSON input.
</info added on 2026-05-20T15:11:02.615Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test representative schemas with valid and invalid payloads and verify inferred types compile in typecheck.

subtasks.title: Integrate schema parsing and middleware into all mutating route handlers
subtasks.description: Update POST, PATCH, and DELETE handlers to use the shared wrapper and perform schema parsing at the top of each handler.
subtasks.details:
Refactor each mutating route to export edge runtime if needed, wrap handlers with withApi, call the appropriate Schema.parse(await req.json()) as the first line inside the handler, and remove duplicated auth/error handling. Ensure validation failures propagate as validation.invalid with HTTP 400 via the shared formatter.
<info added on 2026-05-20T15:12:17.428Z>
Current repo state: there are no custom mutating route handlers yet, except the Auth.js v5 catch-all route (/api/auth/[...nextauth], which uses its own built-in CSRF system and must not be wrapped with withApi) plus GET /api/csrf and GET /api/health. Therefore this subtask does not modify existing routes and only delivers reusable infrastructure.

Delivered behavior/contract:
withApi is exported, and lib/api/middleware.ts includes a usage example at the top.
ZodError -> HTTP 400 validation.invalid is centrally mapped inside withApi; route handlers should just call await parseJson(req, Schema) via the helper from schemas.ts and must not duplicate try/catch for validation.
CSRF enforcement for POST/PATCH/PUT/DELETE is controlled by the MUTATING_METHODS set inside middleware; new mutating routes do not need to call requireCsrf explicitly.

Adoption timing:
Task 7 credential CRUD routes and later R2 operation routes will be the first consumers.

Required route shape for all new mutating endpoints:
export const POST = withApi(async (req, ctx) => { const input = await parseJson(req, Schema); ... })
Keep this convention for PATCH/PUT/DELETE as well so future grep-based boundary audits remain straightforward.

Pitfalls / exclusions:
Do not wrap /api/auth/* with withApi. Auth.js/NextAuth CSRF and this app-level CSRF are two independent token systems; stacking them will conflict, and the Credentials provider already validates its form POST internally.
GET /api/csrf is a bootstrap endpoint and must remain outside withApi. It is GET-only and exists so the client can obtain a CSRF token before mutating requests. Implement it as an inline handler that calls requireSession, sets the CSRF cookie directly, and returns the token.
GET /api/health remains outside this flow.
</info added on 2026-05-20T15:12:17.428Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Route-level tests for representative POST/PATCH/DELETE endpoints verifying valid requests succeed and invalid bodies return 400 with the unified error payload.

subtasks.title: Add client-side CSRF header propagation from cookie
subtasks.description: Ensure the frontend reads the CSRF cookie and includes it in X-CSRF-Token for all mutating API requests.
subtasks.details:
Implement a small client utility or fetch wrapper that reads the csrf cookie value and attaches it to X-CSRF-Token on POST, PATCH, and DELETE requests. Update existing API callers to use the wrapper consistently and document the requirement for future client mutations.
<info added on 2026-05-20T15:12:26.959Z>
Add lib/api/client.ts as the browser-only fetch wrapper used by all TanStack Query mutation hooks, exporting apiFetch, readCookie, ensureCsrfToken, and ApiClientError. apiFetch should infer method, normalize Headers, support a json payload option that auto-serializes and sets content-type, default credentials to "include", and for mutating methods inject X-CSRF-Token by first reading the csrf cookie and lazily bootstrapping GET /api/csrf via ensureCsrfToken when missing. For JSON error responses on 4xx/5xx, parse { error: { code, message, requestId } } and throw ApiClientError carrying those fields; for non-JSON failures throw a normal Error. Return undefined on 204 responses. Keep cookie parsing dependency-free using string splitting, and preserve values containing "=" by joining the remainder after the first split.

Implement app/api/csrf/route.ts as a GET-only edge runtime bootstrap endpoint without withApi. Use explicit try/catch plus toErrorResponse, call requireSession, read the raw csrf token from ctx.csrfToken on the JWT/session context, write the cookie via buildCsrfCookie on the response Set-Cookie header, return { csrfToken, cookieName }, and send cache-control: no-store to prevent intermediaries from caching the cookie bootstrap. If a valid session exists but csrfToken is absent in an older JWT/session, return a 500 internal error instructing the client flow to re-login.

Testing requirements: add tests/unit/api/client.test.ts with 10 cases covering multi-cookie parsing, missing cookie, cookie values containing "=", X-CSRF-Token only on mutating methods, lazy bootstrap through /api/csrf, ApiClientError field mapping, and 204 => undefined. In Vitest, declare fetch mocks with a generic vi.fn<(url, init?) => Promise<Response>> signature to avoid Headers/lib.dom tuple typing issues, stub document via vi.stubGlobal("document", { cookie: ... }) and clean up with vi.unstubAllGlobals(), and define ApiFetchInit so body is explicitly re-added after omitting RequestInit.body to preserve FormData/Blob callers.

tasks.title: Upload Queue store + dispatcher（3×3 并发）
tasks.description: Zustand store 管理上传任务的内存状态机；dispatcher 协程：3 个文件并发，每文件 3 个 part 并发；不持久化（关页面=丢失）；取消即 abort。
tasks.details: 1) stores/upload-queue.ts：task = { id, file, bucket, key, status: 'queued'|'preparing'|'uploading'|'completing'|'done'|'failed'|'canceled', uploadId?, parts: Map<number,{etag?, status}>, progress: {bytesUploaded, totalBytes} }；2) actions: enqueue, cancel, retry, removeDone；3) dispatcher：useEffect 在 layout 启动一个 worker loop，挑 status='queued' 的最早 3 个开跑；4) 单文件 flow：if size < 100MB -> presign put -> fetch PUT；else create multipart -> 切片 10MB part -> 每 part presign upload-part + fetch PUT，3 个并发 -> complete-multipart；5) ETag 从 response header 取（注意 R2 用 ETag header，可能带引号）；6) cancel: AbortController + 调 /api/r2/multipart/abort；7) Progress 用 XMLHttpRequest 而非 fetch（fetch 无上传进度）或用 ReadableStream 上传 + 计算字节数。
tasks.testStrategy: Vitest：单文件 < 100MB 走单 PUT 路径；> 100MB 走 multipart 路径，part 数 = ceil(size/10MB)；并发数测试：5 个文件入队同时只有 3 个进入 uploading；cancel 后 status='canceled' 且调了 abort 端点；E2E：拖 300MB 假文件成功上传到 R2。

tasks.title: 审计日志查看器
tasks.description: GET /api/audit ?cursor=&op=&bucket= 列表分页；UI /(dashboard)/audit 页表格展示。
tasks.details: 1) /api/audit/route.ts (GET)：WHERE user_id=? AND (op=? optional) AND (bucket=? optional) ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 100 + cursor (用 created_at + id 复合 cursor)；2) UI: AuditTable 列 Time / Op (badge) / Bucket / Key / Status / IP；3) Op 下拉筛选（含所有枚举）；Bucket 输入框筛选；4) 不做导出（V1）。
tasks.testStrategy: Vitest：分页 cursor 正确；筛选 op='object.delete' 只返回对应行；E2E：执行一次删除后审计页能看到该记录。

tasks.title: Vitest 单元测试套件
tasks.description: 覆盖 crypto/auth/r2/api/audit/rate-limit 核心模块，目标 80%+ 行覆盖；route handler 用 mock D1 + mock S3 测。
tasks.details: 1) vitest.config.ts：environment='node'（edge runtime 用 miniflare 适配器更佳，可装 @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers）；2) 用 better-sqlite3 in-memory 跑 drizzle 迁移作为 D1 替身；3) S3Client 用 aws-sdk-client-mock 包；4) tests/unit/crypto/aes-gcm.test.ts、auth/password.test.ts、api/csrf.test.ts、api/rate-limit.test.ts、r2/presign.test.ts、r2/control.test.ts、api/connections.test.ts、api/presign.test.ts、api/delete.test.ts；5) coverage 通过 v8 provider；6) CI 时 typecheck + lint + test 串行。
tasks.testStrategy: `pnpm test` 通过；`pnpm test --coverage` 行覆盖 ≥80%（crypto 和 api 模块 ≥95%）。

tasks.title: Playwright E2E 关键流 + 部署/CORS 文档
tasks.description: Playwright 覆盖：登录、添加 connection、浏览桶、上传（小/大）、下载、删除（typed 确认）、生成分享。CLAUDE.md 补 Security Invariant #3 豁免脚注；README 加 R2 CORS 配置示例 + multipart lifecycle 提示。
tasks.details: 1) playwright.config.ts：webServer 跑 `pnpm preview`（wrangler 本地 D1）；baseURL=http://localhost:8788；2) tests/e2e/login.spec.ts、connections.spec.ts、browse-upload-download.spec.ts、delete.spec.ts、share.spec.ts；3) fixtures：登录态可复用 storageState；4) 用真实 R2 测试桶（CI 用一个专用 throwaway 桶，凭证存 GH Secrets）或 minio 本地 S3 兼容服务；5) 文档：docs/r2-cors.md 给出标准 CORS JSON（AllowedOrigins=[NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL], AllowedMethods=[GET, PUT], AllowedHeaders=[*], MaxAgeSeconds=3600）；6) docs/multipart-cleanup.md 推荐 R2 lifecycle rule 'AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload after 7 days'；7) CLAUDE.md Security Invariant #3 加脚注：'Exception: control-plane R2 calls (list, delete, create/complete/abort multipart upload) MAY be made server-side as they only exchange metadata, not object bytes.'；8) 部署 runbook：env 配置（ENCRYPTION_KEY=`openssl rand -base64 32`）、首次 admin seed 步骤、D1 migration 应用、wrangler pages deploy。
tasks.testStrategy: `pnpm test:e2e` 全部通过；本地 `pnpm preview` 能完整走通 login -> upload 100MB 文件 -> 删除 -> 分享；wrangler pages deploy --dry-run 通过；CLAUDE.md 修改后 grep 验证脚注存在；README 含 CORS 示例。
